book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon V.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Let none of those who are on the priestly list possess any woman or maid servant, beyond those who are enumerated in the canon as being persons free from suspicion, preserving himself hereby from being implicated in any blame.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But if anyone transgresses our decree let him be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: And let eunuchs also observe the same rule, that by foresight they may be free of censure.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But those who transgress, let them be deposed, if indeed they are clerics; but if laymen let them be excommunicated.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: A priest, even if a eunuch, shall not have in his house a maid or other woman except those on whom no suspicion can light.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: See Canon III., of First Ecumenical Council at Nice.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
